title: interfaces_with relation (ONT-R-15, D-05)
description: Peer-tier system↔system interface (data, power, mechanical, thermal). USE FOR: AvionicsBay interfaces_with ECS via ARINC 429; HydraulicSystem interfaces_with FlightControlSystem via servo actuators; Subsystem(power_distribution) interfaces_with Subsystem(generator). DO NOT USE FOR: parent-child (use part_of); cross-tier dependency (use requires); regulatory compliance (use complies_with). Boundary with 'requires' per ADR-003 / D-10: 'requires' is cross-tier (Component requires MaintenanceTask, SimulationCase requires MeshRequirement); 'interfaces_with' is peer-tier between two systems/subsystems at the same architectural level. Order of subject/object is symbolic only — interfaces_with is logically symmetric but stored asymmetrically for query simplicity. H-Darrieus relevance: an interfaces_with edge between two AI-extracted systems with confidence > 0.85 must have a reviewer per the validator rule (Phase 3). Schema version: 0.1.0.

interface_type.description: Nature of the interface. 'data' covers ARINC 429 / AFDX / discrete signals; 'power' covers electrical bus connections; 'mechanical' covers structural attachment / linkage; 'thermal' covers heat exchange paths; 'fluid' covers hydraulic / pneumatic plumbing; 'control' covers command/feedback loops; 'other' for anything outside the above. Required.

interface_protocol.description: Optional. Free-text or standard reference for the interface protocol (e.g. 'ARINC 429', 'AFDX', 'MIL-STD-1553', 'CAN bus', 'servo actuator'). Phase 4 demo data SHOULD set this where applicable.
